summary: Reasonable basic test suite with good organization and structure. Tests 11 scenarios covering the main features: cartesian product generation (2D, 3D), include/exclude rules, option passthrough (max-parallel, fail-fast), size validation, error handling, and default behavior. Tests are well-organized with clear section comments and proper fixture isolation via setup(). Main weaknesses: (1) stdin input feature (mentioned in script with '-' support) has no test; (2) error validation is shallow—only checks exit code != 0 and grep for substring, not exact error messages or codes; (3) limited edge case coverage—no tests for single-value dimensions, empty include/exclude arrays, max-size boundary conditions (=0, =1, exact match), partial exclude rules matching, multiple exclude rules, or overlapping include/exclude combinations; (4) no inline comments explaining assertion intent; (5) fixture contents not documented. Would catch basic regressions in core happy path but miss edge cases and subtle failures. For a shell test suite, decent basic coverage but lacks rigor expected of comprehensive suite.
